title: Frequent presymptomatic household transmission of influenza A but not influenza B virus
methods: covariate modelling of susceptibility and infectiousness
incubation period and infectiousness profile estimation
data_sources: Hong Kong influenza A household cohort studies (2008-2017)
Hong Kong influenza B household cohort studies (2008-2017)
Hong Kong SARS-CoV-2 Omicron household dataset (validation, restricted)

data_scope.data_availability: influenza A and B data publicly available at https://github.com/ZhangCYTian/presymptomatic_household; SARS-CoV-2 data require IRB approval

core_conclusion: Influenza A transmits presymptomatically at a modest but significant level (9.6%, 95% CrI 5.9%-14.7%), while influenza B shows no evidence of presymptomatic transmission. Validation against SARS-CoV-2 Omicron (65.5% presymptomatic) confirms the method's reliability. Symptom-based case isolation is a viable strategy for influenza, with limited marginal benefit from quarantining presymptomatic contacts.
why_it_matters: Provides first rigorous pathogen-specific presymptomatic transmission estimates for seasonal influenza; reveals a striking biological difference between influenza A and B; directly quantifies the policy value of presymptomatic contact quarantine; introduces a reusable Bayesian framework for cross-pathogen comparisons; data and code are fully open.
why_nature: Resolves a decades-old public-health uncertainty with a rigorous methodological advance; cross-pathogen scope delivers generalizable insights; findings translate directly into isolation and quarantine policy design; validated against an independent pandemic dataset (SARS-CoV-2 Omicron).
added_value_to_my_research: Couple presymptomatic transmission rates with spatiotemporal mobility data to produce neighbourhood-level influenza A exposure risk maps
Apply Bayesian latent-variable framework to contact-tracing records for spatially explicit presymptomatic exposure mapping across healthcare catchment areas
Integrate pathogen-specific presymptomatic parameters into agent-based NPI simulators to model heterogeneous quarantine-policy impacts across urban sub-populations
Link presymptomatic influenza spread to healthcare accessibility surfaces (2SFCA) to identify high-risk zones where early spread would go undetected
Adapt the individual-based model to H5N1 spillover data and overlay with spatial accessibility analysis to prioritize surveillance in low-access poultry-farming regions

related.title: Impacts of human mobility on the citywide transmission dynamics of 18 respiratory viruses in pre- and post-COVID-19 pandemic years
related.relation: provides mobility-stratified respiratory virus transmission framework to extend household model to community scale

related.title: Modelling the impact of testing, contact tracing and household quarantine on second waves of COVID-19
related.relation: NPI evaluation framework into which presymptomatic transmission parameters can be fed to simulate quarantine policy scenarios

related.title: Incidence and transmission of respiratory syncytial virus in urban and rural South Africa, 2017-2018
related.relation: parallel household cohort transmission study for RSV enabling cross-pathogen co-infection analysis

related.title: Contact tracing reveals community transmission of COVID-19 in New York City
related.relation: demonstrates use of contact-tracing records to reconstruct transmission chains, enabling scale-up of the household model

related.title: Containment scenarios for post-spillover transmission chains of avian influenza H5N1 from poultry to humans
related.relation: agent-based model for H5N1 containment that would benefit from presymptomatic parameter estimates derived by this paper's framework

related.title: Competition between transmission lineages mediated by human mobility shapes seasonal influenza epidemics in the US
related.relation: spatial influenza transmission dynamics providing population-level context for household-derived presymptomatic estimates
